content.problemStatement:
Go through the code in your editor slowly to see what’s causing the problem and fix it.

For a given integer, we need to print an integer twice the input. 

Check the sample input / output below for further clarity.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the value 2 × N.

solution.approach: The problem asks for doubling each given integer. For each test case we simply read N and output N*2. This is a direct O(1) operation per test case, so the total time is O(T). No extra data structures are needed.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides input and output buffers

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[]args)throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int T=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int i=0;i<T;i++){String line=br.readLine();while(line!=null&&line.isEmpty()){line=br.readLine();}int N=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());sb.append(N*2);if(i<T-1)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
